# XIRI Facility Solutions — Complete Reference

> XIRI provides managed facility maintenance for single-tenant NNN buildings — medical offices, labs, auto dealerships, and commercial facilities in Nassau County, Long Island, NY. One partner. One invoice. Nightly verified.

Founded by Chris Leung in Long Island, NY. XIRI manages the full facility maintenance lifecycle: vendor vetting, scheduling, quality verification (via NFC tap-to-verify), invoicing, and compliance documentation.

Website: https://xiri.ai
Contact: chris@xiri.ai
Service Area: Nassau County, Long Island, New York

---

## How XIRI Works

1. **Site Audit** — We walk your facility, document every surface, fixture, and compliance requirement
2. **Custom Scope** — Build a cleaning specification tailored to your facility type and regulations
3. **Contractor Match** — Assign vetted, insured contractors from our network
4. **NFC Verification** — Cleaners tap NFC tags at each zone to prove work was completed
5. **Single Invoice** — One monthly invoice covers all services, all vendors, all locations

---

## Janitorial Cleaning Cost Data (2026)

### Cost Per Square Foot by Facility Type

| Facility Type | $/sqft/visit | Monthly (10,000 sqft, 5x/week) | Notes |
|--------------|:------------:|:------------------------------:|-------|
| General Office | $0.08–$0.18 | $2,000–$3,600 | Most common |
| Medical Office | $0.15–$0.25 | $3,200–$5,700 | OSHA disinfection required |
| Dental Office | $0.14–$0.22 | $2,800–$5,000 | CDC surface protocols |
| Auto Dealership | $0.10–$0.20 | $2,400–$4,800 | Showroom + service bay |
| Daycare | $0.12–$0.20 | $2,400–$4,500 | Green Seal products required |
| Surgery Center | $0.18–$0.30 | $3,600–$6,500 | Terminal cleaning nightly |
| Retail Store | $0.06–$0.12 | $1,200–$2,400 | High foot traffic |
| Fitness/Gym | $0.10–$0.18 | $2,000–$3,600 | Locker room focus |

### Average Monthly Costs by Building Size

| Building Size | Monthly Cost Range | Assumption |
|:------------:|:-----------------:|:----------:|
| 2,000–5,000 sqft | $600–$1,500/mo | 5x/week, general office |
| 5,000–15,000 sqft | $1,500–$4,000/mo | 5x/week, mid-cost state |
| 15,000–50,000+ sqft | $3,500–$10,000+/mo | 5x/week, scope-dependent |

### Factors That Affect Cleaning Cost

1. **Facility type** — Medical offices take ~2x longer per sqft than general offices due to OSHA disinfection
2. **Floor surfaces** — Carpet is fastest; tile/stone with grout lines is slowest. A 100% carpet facility costs 15–25% less than hard floors
3. **Fixture count** — Each restroom fixture adds ~3 minutes; each trash can adds ~1 minute
4. **Cleaning frequency** — 3x/week saves 30–40% vs 5x/week, but each visit costs more
5. **State labor rates** — NY ($20/hr min wage) costs ~60% more than federal minimum ($7.25/hr) states
6. **Add-on services** — Window cleaning, floor stripping, pressure washing quoted separately

### Pricing Methodology

XIRI uses production-rate-based pricing — the same method professional cleaning companies use to build bids. We calculate:
- **Base hours** = Square footage ÷ Production rate (sqft/hour for your facility type)
- **Fixture hours** = (Restroom fixtures × 3 min + Trash bins × 1 min) ÷ 60
- **Floor adjustment** = Weighted multiplier based on your carpet/resilient/tile mix
- **Total cost** = (Base hours + Fixture hours) × Floor adjustment × State hourly rate

Free calculator: https://xiri.ai/calculator

---

## Core Services

- **Janitorial Services** — Recurring nightly/weekly cleaning for commercial facilities
- **Medical Office Cleaning** — JCAHO-compliant with terminal disinfection and documentation
- **Commercial Cleaning** — Full-service for offices, retail, and professional suites
- **Floor Care** — Strip, seal, and maintain VCT, LVT, epoxy, and concrete flooring
- **Disinfecting Services** — EPA-registered disinfection for healthcare environments
- **HVAC Maintenance** — Preventive service for single-tenant commercial buildings
- **Window Cleaning** — Interior and exterior for commercial facilities
- **Pressure Washing** — Parking lots, sidewalks, building facades
- **Pest Control** — Licensed management for medical, food service, and commercial
- **Waste Management** — Regulated and general waste including red-bag medical waste
- **Snow & Ice Removal** — Winter weather response for lots, walkways, entrances
- **Handyman Services** — General maintenance and repair
- **Parking Lot Maintenance** — Sweeping, striping, surface repair
- **Post-Construction Cleanup** — Final clean and punch-list support

---

## Industries Served

- **Medical Offices** — JCAHO-compliant, OSHA bloodborne pathogen standards
- **Surgery Centers** — Terminal cleaning, sterile environment maintenance
- **Dental Offices** — CDC-aligned sanitization protocols
- **Urgent Care Centers** — High-traffic clinical cleaning
- **Dialysis Centers** — Infection control protocols
- **Veterinary Clinics** — Biohazard handling
- **Auto Dealerships** — Showroom, service bay, lot maintenance
- **Daycares & Preschools** — Child-safe, non-toxic products (Green Seal certified)
- **Labs & Cleanrooms** — ISO 14644-1 compliant maintenance
- **Light Manufacturing** — FOD prevention, ESD-safe cleaning
- **Professional Offices** — General office cleaning
- **Fitness Centers** — Gym and locker room sanitation

---

## Specialized Solutions

- **JCAHO Survey-Ready Disinfection** — Audit-ready protocols for medical facilities
- **Terminal Cleaning for Surgery Centers** — End-of-day sterile field cleaning
- **Dental Suite Sanitization** — CDC-aligned sterilization support
- **ISO 7/8 Cleanroom Protocol** — Particle control and gowning protocols
- **cGMP Lab Decontamination** — FDA-grade decontamination
- **BSL-2 Lab Waste Management** — Biosafety-compliant waste handling
- **ESD-Safe Floor Care** — Electrostatic discharge prevention for electronics manufacturing
- **Auto Shop Slip-Coefficient Logging** — NYS Part 226 compliant floor safety documentation
- **Data Center Zinc-Whisker Remediation** — Sub-floor contamination removal

---

## Compliance & Regulatory Expertise

XIRI maintains compliance documentation for:
- **OSHA** — Bloodborne pathogen standards (29 CFR 1910.1030)
- **JCAHO** — Joint Commission environmental cleaning standards
- **HIPAA** — Protected health information in cleaning waste
- **CMS** — Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services facility requirements
- **CDC** — Surface disinfection guidelines for healthcare
- **EPA** — Registered disinfectant usage and documentation
- **ISO 14644-1** — Cleanroom classification and maintenance
- **FDA cGMP** — Current Good Manufacturing Practice for labs
- **NYS Part 226** — Slip-coefficient floor safety standards

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How much does janitorial cleaning cost?
Janitorial cleaning costs typically range from $0.05 to $0.25 per square foot per visit, or $1,200 to $4,500 per month for a 10,000 sqft facility cleaned 5x/week. The exact cost depends on facility type, location, cleaning frequency, floor surfaces, and number of restrooms.

### How much do janitorial services cost per month?
A small office (2,000–5,000 sqft) typically costs $600–$1,500/month. A mid-size facility (5,000–15,000 sqft) runs $1,500–$4,000/month. Large facilities (15,000–50,000+ sqft) range from $3,500–$10,000+/month. These assume 5x/week cleaning in a mid-cost state.

### Is it cheaper to hire a cleaner or a janitorial service?
A janitorial service is typically more cost-effective for facilities under 50,000 sqft. An in-house cleaner costs $35,000–$50,000/year plus benefits, workers comp, and equipment — roughly $4,000–$6,000/month all-in. A professional service for 10,000 sqft runs $2,000–$4,000/month and includes trained staff, supplies, insurance, and backup coverage.

### What's the difference between janitorial and commercial cleaning?
Janitorial cleaning is recurring routine maintenance — daily trash, vacuuming, restroom cleaning, surface wiping. Commercial cleaning is broader, including periodic services like carpet extraction, floor stripping/waxing, window washing, and pressure washing.

### How is janitorial cleaning priced?
Professional services are priced by estimated hours per visit, calculated from square footage, production rate for facility type, fixture counts, floor types, and add-on services. The hourly rate varies by state based on local labor costs.

### Why does facility type affect cleaning cost?
Medical offices need OSHA-compliant disinfection and take nearly twice as long per sqft as general offices. Auto showrooms require high-gloss floor care. Daycares need child-safe, Green Seal certified products. Each facility type has different production rates reflecting real-world cleaning requirements.

---

## For Contractors

XIRI operates a vetted contractor network. Contractors receive:
- Steady, recurring facility assignments
- XIRI handles client billing — contractors get paid on schedule
- NFC-based work verification (no manual reporting)
- Insurance and compliance documentation support

Apply: https://xiri.ai/contractors

## Service Area

Primary service area: Nassau County, Long Island, New York
Key corridors: Great Neck Medical Mile (Northern Blvd), New Hyde Park / LIJ Medical Center, Mineola, Garden City, Freeport, Hempstead

---

## Key Differentiators

1. **NFC Tap-to-Verify** — Every cleaning zone has an NFC tag. Cleaners must physically tap each zone to confirm work completion. This provides real-time proof of service.
2. **Single Invoice** — Facilities get one monthly invoice covering all services (janitorial, HVAC, pest control, snow removal, etc.) regardless of how many contractors are involved.
3. **Production-Rate Pricing** — Transparent, data-driven pricing based on industry-standard production rates — not guesswork.
4. **Compliance-First** — Every contractor is vetted for insurance, training certifications, and regulatory knowledge specific to your facility type.
5. **Nightly Verified** — Digital cleaning verification reports available to facility managers every morning.
